title: Missing bounds check before TLV field read in loadBinaryMessage causes out-of-bounds crash
description: In loadBinaryMessage(), the while loop reads field IDs at offset..offset+4 (line 468) without first checking that offset+4 <= data.count. If the message data is truncated or malformed (e.g., only 2 bytes remaining), data.subdata(in: offset..<offset+4) will crash with a range out-of-bounds fatal error. Similarly, the field length read at line 486 (data.subdata(in: offset..<offset+4)) lacks a guard that offset+4 <= data.count. The field value read at line 498 (data.subdata(in: offset..<offset+fieldLength)) lacks a guard that offset+fieldLength <= data.count. Any of these can be triggered by a malicious peer sending a truncated binary message.
recommendation: Add bounds checks before each subdata call: guard offset + 4 <= data.count else { Logger.error("Truncated field"); return } before reading field ID and field length, and guard offset + fieldLength <= data.count else { return } before reading field value.
